tasks_completed.description: BUG 1: Fix _process_shell_interp to detect 'c' anywhere in short-option cluster
tasks_completed.changes: Line 298: replaced text.strip() == '-c' with compound predicate: token starts with - or + (not --) AND 'c' in token[1:]. Handles -lc, -ce, -xc, +c, +ec and all POSIX combined-flag forms.
tasks_completed.rationale: Exact equality == '-c' never matches combined-flag tokens; POSIX specifies -c as option that can appear anywhere in a cluster.

tasks_completed.description: BUG 2: Fix _find_command_word to skip sudo option-argument pairs
tasks_completed.changes: Added module-level _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG frozenset. After detecting sudo in _WRAPPER_PREFIXES, added inner loop that consumes: (a) options in _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG + their value, (b) -- terminator (break), (c) any other -/+ flag (no-arg skip). Does not over-skip: -H is not in _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG so bash is correctly returned as command word.
tasks_completed.rationale: Original code skipped only the sudo token itself; -u root caused -u to be returned as command word rather than bash.

tasks_completed.description: BUG 3: Fix _process_heredocs pipe-to-shell detection for absolute-path shells
tasks_completed.changes: Removed the literal-name regex r'\|\s*(bash|sh|zsh|dash)\b' entirely (it matched | bash.old via \b boundary). Replaced with: any(_first_word(seg) in _SHELL_INTERPS for seg in after.split('|')[1:]). Splits after on '|', checks _first_word of each post-pipe segment which calls _basename() internally.
tasks_completed.rationale: The regex had a false-positive: \b matches between 'h' and '.' so '| bash.old' was incorrectly detected as shell context. The _first_word()/_basename() approach correctly rejects 'bash.old' (not in _SHELL_INTERPS) while accepting '/bin/bash' (basename='bash', in _SHELL_INTERPS).

tasks_completed.description: M4: Add regression tests for all 3 bugs covering AC1-AC8 and AC10
tasks_completed.changes: Added 3 test classes: TestBug1CombinedFlagWithC (3 tests: AC1, AC2, AC10), TestBug2WrapperCommandOptions (3 tests: AC3, AC4, AC5), TestBug3AbsolutePathShellInPipe (4 tests: AC6, AC7, AC8, regex false-positive regression). Total: 10 new tests.
tasks_completed.rationale: M4 requirement; security regression tests ensure the three bypass paths cannot re-open silently.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — pre-existing precision gaps in original bash_context_strip.py implementation
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Module written as a conservative non-full-parser; three code paths made simplifying assumptions (exact token match, single-token wrapper skip, literal-name regex) that hold for common cases but fail for POSIX combined-flag and absolute-path shell forms.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Each fix replaces the simplifying assumption with a correct semantic check: (1) 'c' membership test on option cluster instead of equality; (2) explicit arity table for sudo options instead of single-token skip; (3) _basename()+_SHELL_INTERPS membership instead of regex name match.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 80 of the 107 lines are test code required by M4 (10 new test cases). Production code changes are 29 lines (27 added + 2 removed), which is the minimum to correctly address all 3 bugs with the _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG constant. No refactoring or scope creep.

dev.qa_notes: Run: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v (expect 40 passed). Also verify AC verification commands in acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-A.json pass. Note: BUG 3 fix removed the regex fast-path entirely (not just augmented it) because the regex had a false-positive on '| bash.old' via \b word boundary. The pipe_bash existing test (test_pipe_bash_consumer_heredoc_preserved) continues to pass with the new implementation.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex found 1 in-scope real bug: the legacy regex fast-path r'\|\s*(bash|sh|zsh|dash)\b' in BUG 3 fix has a false-positive on '| bash.old' because \b matches between 'h' and '.'. Codex also found 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY: sudo arity table is incomplete for -p/-A/-h/-T options (out of scope per BA spec C1 deferral class).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied Codex fix: removed legacy regex entirely; shell_ctx now uses only the _first_word()+_SHELL_INTERPS membership approach. Added test_bare_bash_dot_old_does_not_preserve_body to catch this regression. The OBSERVATION_ONLY finding about sudo arity table incompleteness is documented but not fixed (out of scope per ticket Won't Have section).

self_verification.notes: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py -v: 40 passed in 0.05s. All 10 AC verification commands from acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-A.json verified to pass against the implementation.

recommendations: Out of scope (C1 deferred): extend _SUDO_OPTS_WITH_ARG to cover -p, -A, -h, -T; add similar arity tables for env -i, timeout <n>, nice -n <n>, ionice -c <n>, doas, run0
Out of scope (C1 deferred): sudo --user=<arg> inline form (no space) is not currently skipped — same bypass class as -u root
